vulnerabilities.description: Port 21 is open and running the 'ftp' service. Unnecessary open ports expand the external attack surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict access via firewalls or Security Groups. Close the port if the service is not strictly required for public operation.

vulnerabilities.description: Port 22 is open and running the 'ssh' service. Unnecessary open ports expand the external attack surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict access via firewalls or Security Groups. Close the port if the service is not strictly required for public operation.

vulnerabilities.description: Port 80 is open and running the 'http' service. Unnecessary open ports expand the external attack surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict access via firewalls or Security Groups. Close the port if the service is not strictly required for public operation.

vulnerabilities.description: Port 443 is open and running the 'https' service. Unnecessary open ports expand the external attack surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict access via firewalls or Security Groups. Close the port if the service is not strictly required for public operation.

vulnerabilities.description: Port 8080 is open and running the 'http-proxy' service. Unnecessary open ports expand the external attack surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict access via firewalls or Security Groups. Close the port if the service is not strictly required for public operation.

vulnerabilities.description: The server does not enforce HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS). This leaves users vulnerable to man-in-the-middle (MITM) downgrade attacks.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure the web server to include the 'Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains' header.

vulnerabilities.description: The 'X-Frame-Options' header is missing, meaning this website can be embedded in an iframe on a malicious site to trick users into clicking buttons.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement 'X-Frame-Options: DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' in your server response headers.
